params.name: Night Watch
params.description:
On Feb 2, 1642 AD, Rembrandt painted the Night Watch for Captain Banninck.
The Night Watch depicts the captain of the guard as he leads his yellow-clad lieutenant as they round up the uniformed ranks. The illusionism and the sense of theatricality and movement in the painting are enforced by the choreography of gestures, glances, muskets, and banners and by the building up of pigment in the foreground that flattens as the perspective recedes. By mixing charged symbolism and reality as well as …
